# Project Guidelines

## Overview

NEXURADATA — bilingual (FR primary, EN secondary) marketing site and operational platform for a data-recovery / digital-forensics lab in Montreal. Stack: Cloudflare Pages + Pages Functions, D1 (SQLite), Stripe, Resend.

See `README.md` for full architecture, prerequisites, and Cloudflare Pages setup.

## Architecture

| Layer | Location | Purpose |
|-------|----------|---------|
| Public site (FR) | `*.html` at root | Marketing + intake forms |
| Public site (EN) | `en/*.html` | English mirrors — same filename as FR counterpart |
| Shared assets | `assets/css/site.css`, `assets/js/site.js` | Styles + interactions |
| Client portal | `suivi-dossier-client-montreal.html` | Case tracking (`noindex`) |
| Operator console | `operations/index.html` | Internal, protected via Cloudflare Access |
| API functions | `functions/api/` | `intake.js`, `status.js`, `stripe-webhook.js` + `ops/` |
| Shared function logic | `functions/_lib/` | D1 queries, email, rate-limiting, HTTP helpers |
| D1 schema | `migrations/` | Incremental migrations (`0001_launch.sql` …); schema reference |
| Config | `wrangler.jsonc` | Source of truth for D1 bindings, env vars, compat date |
| Build output | `release-cloudflare/` | **Never edit** — regenerated by `npm run build` |

## Build and Test

```bash
npm run build              # regenerate release-cloudflare/
npm run cf:dev             # local dev with Functions + D1 (runs build first)
npm run cf:d1:migrate:local  # apply D1 migrations locally
npm test                   # vitest unit tests (tests/ directory)
```

See `README.md` for the full command list (`cf:deploy`, `cf:deploy:staging`, etc.).

## Critical Gotchas

- **Never edit `release-cloudflare/`** — it is regenerated by `npm run build`. Edit source files at root.
- **Bilingual pages**: FR at root, EN under `en/`, same filename. Always update **both** when changing shared content or structure.
- **Secrets** (`RESEND_API_KEY`, `ACCESS_CODE_SECRET`, `STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`) must never appear in code or logs.
- **`wrangler.jsonc`** is the single source of truth for D1 bindings, env vars, and compatibility date.
- **Functions use ESM** (`export const onRequest…` / `export const onRequestPost…`) — no CommonJS.
- **`functions/_lib/`** — reuse these helpers instead of duplicating logic in new functions.
- **Operator routes** (`/api/ops/*`, `/operations/*`) must be protected by Cloudflare Access — never add auth bypass.

## Specialized Instructions (auto-applied)

These `.instructions.md` files activate automatically for matching file patterns:
- `.github/instructions/branding.instructions.md` — logo, header, footer, nav, visual tokens
- `.github/instructions/cloudflare-functions.instructions.md` — `functions/**/*.js`
- `.github/instructions/d1-migrations.instructions.md` — `migrations/**/*.sql`
- `.github/instructions/design-system.instructions.md` — HTML/CSS/JS authoring

## Key Documentation

- `README.md` — structure, commands, Cloudflare Pages setup.
- `LAUNCH-RUNBOOK.md` — step-by-step launch (D1, email, Stripe, Access).
- `DEPLOY-FAST.md` — quick deployment options.
- `LAUNCH-CHECKLIST.md` — pre-launch content items.
- `docs/branding-read-only.txt` — locked brand identity (do not alter).
